consistency.general: The data structure is consistent with typical spell formatting, containing all necessary mechanical and descriptive attributes.
consistency.mechanics: The mechanics (casting time, range, saving throw, effect description, duration, and scaling) are internally consistent. The d10 table clearly defines the random effects imposed on affected creatures.

quality.completeness: The entry is complete, including level, school, components, and class availability.
quality.readability: The description is well-formatted, clearly separating the initial effect, the turn-by-turn behavior table, and the ending condition.

mechanics_review.primary_effect: Forces creatures in an area to make a Wisdom save or be subjected to mental manipulation for up to 1 minute (concentration).
mechanics_review.saving_throw: Wisdom save to resist initially, and a Wisdom save at the end of each turn to potentially end the effect.
mechanics_review.behavior_table_evaluation: The d10 table provides a good range of outcomes: 10% random movement, 50% inaction (2-6), 20% random attack (7-8), and 20% normal action (9-10). The 2-6 result (50% chance of doing nothing) is a substantial drawback for the affected creature.
mechanics_review.scaling: Scaling is standard for area-of-effect spells, increasing the radius by 5 feet per spell level above 4th (e.g., 15ft at 5th, 20ft at 6th, etc.).
mechanics_review.components: V, S, M (three nut shells) is standard for an enchantment spell of this level.

analysis.issues_found: No critical errors or inconsistencies were found. The spell functions as described within standard TTRPG rulesets (D&D 5e). The mechanical details are robust.
